steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The word “but” signals a contrast between how the reasons appear and a warning about overlooking important nuances. For the contrast to work effectively, the blank needs to describe reasons that seem compelling or sound.

steps.title: Apply to the choices
steps.content: ”Irrefutable” (meaning undeniably true) works because describing the reasons as compelling makes the “but” a genuine counter-argument—the characterization seems valid initially, but the author warns it risks overlooking subtleties. The other choices already signal problems with the reasons, so they fail to create the contrast that “but” requires. That matches choice D — D is correct.
